pi_items.verified_value: food.gdt - 40 household observations with food_exp and income variables
pi_items.source: Hill, Griffiths and Lim, Principles of Econometrics 5th Edition (https://principlesofeconometrics.com/poe5/poe5.html); learneconometrics.com
Task description and setup_task.sh line 9, export_result.sh line 12

Calculated from food.gdt data using OLS regression (ln(food_exp) = 3.8364 + 0.6488*ln(income)) with 15% income increase scenario yielding aggregate increase of 957.82; tolerance of 10.0 is 1.04% of ground truth

Computed from food.gdt (40 observations). OLS: ln(food_exp) = 3.836443 + 0.648770*ln(income). Baseline food expenditure sum = 10164.15. With 15% income increase, new total = 11121.97, aggregate increase = 957.82

privileged_info_summary: The food.gdt dataset contains real household survey data from Hill, Griffiths, and Lim's Principles of Econometrics (5th Edition), consisting of 40 household observations of weekly food expenditure and weekly income. The double-log Engel curve model produces OLS estimates of intercept b0=3.836443 and income elasticity b1=0.648770. A 15% income increase scenario yields a ground truth aggregate food expenditure increase of 957.82, with a tolerance of 10.0 (approximately 1% of the expected value). The data is real, not synthetic.
